text: BlueRiver Bancorp reported first-quarter profit up 19%, beating analyst estimates on stronger loan growth.
Crestline Media's quarterly revenue rose 15%, topping forecasts, as streaming subscriptions hit a record.
Solaris Materials raised its full-year earnings guidance after a stronger-than-expected second quarter.
Northwind Semiconductors reported a 12% drop in quarterly revenue and guided below consensus for the current quarter.
Helios Renewables swung to a quarterly loss as project delays pushed revenue below expectations.
Vertex Logistics' profit fell 30% last quarter, missing estimates, on higher fuel and labor costs.
Meridian Health will hold its quarterly earnings call on Thursday at 5 p.m. Eastern.
Apex Retail reported quarterly results in line with analyst expectations, with revenue flat year over year.
Solaris Materials filed its annual report, restating segment figures with no change to consolidated totals.
Apex Retail agreed to buy online marketplace ShopHive for $1.8 billion, expanding its e-commerce reach.
Shares of Crestline Media surged on reports that a private equity consortium is preparing a buyout offer.
BlueRiver Bancorp's acquisition of a regional lender closed ahead of schedule and is expected to add to earnings in year one.
Vertex Logistics walked away from its planned merger with FreightLink, forfeiting a $200 million breakup fee.
Helios Renewables' takeover of VoltCore faces an extended regulatory review, delaying closing by up to a year.
Meridian Health completed the previously announced sale of its dental unit; terms were unchanged from the July agreement.
Northwind Semiconductors said it routinely evaluates acquisition opportunities and declined to comment on deal speculation.
Regulators approved BlueRiver Bancorp's plan to resume share buybacks after it passed the annual stress test.
The environmental agency granted Helios Renewables permits for its two largest solar projects, clearing the path to construction.
Meridian Health received a warning letter from the FDA over manufacturing practices at its Ohio plant.
A court ruled against Northwind Semiconductors in a patent dispute, exposing it to up to $900 million in damages.
Apex Retail faces a class-action lawsuit alleging it mislabeled product origins across its private-label brands.
The securities regulator opened a routine comment period on proposed disclosure rules for mid-cap issuers.
Crestline Media said it is cooperating with a regulatory inquiry into industry-wide ad-measurement practices.
Cooling inflation data fueled a broad market rally, with retail and consumer stocks among the biggest gainers.
Strong jobs numbers and resilient consumer spending lifted forecasts for fourth-quarter GDP growth.
Manufacturing activity contracted for the fifth consecutive month, deepening fears of an industrial recession.
The central bank signaled rates will stay higher for longer, pressuring rate-sensitive sectors.
A stronger dollar is expected to shave several points off multinational earnings this quarter, strategists warned.
The treasury will auction $60 billion in 10-year notes next week, in line with the previous schedule.
Economists are split on whether the central bank will cut rates at its December meeting.
Meridian Health's new glucose monitor won strong early reviews, with preorders exceeding internal targets.
Northwind's latest server processor secured design wins at three of the five largest cloud providers.
Apex Retail's loyalty app topped download charts within a week of launch, driving record holiday foot traffic.
Crestline Media shut down its short-form video app two years after launch, citing weak engagement.
Solaris Materials halted shipments of a key coating product after customers reported quality defects.
Helios Renewables will begin taking orders for its commercial battery system in the fall.
Vertex Logistics is piloting autonomous delivery vans in two cities, with results expected next year.
Solaris Materials announced a $2 billion share buyback and a 10% dividend increase.
Veteran operator Dana Whitfield was named chief executive of Vertex Logistics, a hire investors welcomed.
BlueRiver Bancorp's chief financial officer resigned amid an internal accounting review.
Crestline Media was dropped from the midcap index following its market-value decline.
Northwind Semiconductors will hold its annual shareholder meeting virtually on May 12.
Apex Retail appointed two new independent directors to its board, effective immediately.
Helios Renewables beat quarterly estimates on surging battery sales and announced a $500 million buyback.
Meridian Health lowered guidance, blaming new reimbursement rules that squeeze margins on implants.
Northwind warned that new export restrictions on advanced chips will cut its data-center revenue by up to 8%.
BlueRiver Bancorp raised its net interest income outlook, citing the higher-rate environment.
Vertex Logistics acquired a customs-brokerage firm and launched a cross-border shipping product built on it.
Northwind Semiconductors posted record third-quarter revenue of $2.1 billion, up 24% year over year, and raised full-year guidance.
Meridian Health cut its annual profit forecast after quarterly earnings missed estimates on weaker elective-procedure volumes.
Apex Retail Group will report fourth-quarter results on March 4 before markets open.
Helios Renewables agreed to acquire battery maker VoltCore for $3.2 billion, a deal analysts say strengthens its storage pipeline.
Antitrust regulators sued to block Vertex Logistics' proposed merger with FreightLink, citing reduced competition on key routes.
Crestline Media confirmed it has held preliminary talks about a possible sale of its podcast unit; no decision has been made.
BlueRiver Bancorp was fined $450 million by regulators over compliance failures in its anti-money-laundering program.
The FDA granted Meridian Health's cardiac implant fast-track designation, shortening its expected approval timeline.
New capital-requirement rules for mid-size lenders take effect in January; BlueRiver said it already meets the thresholds.
Shares of Apex Retail fell after data showed consumer spending contracted for a third straight month amid persistent inflation.
The central bank's surprise rate cut lifted rate-sensitive stocks, with homebuilders and utilities rallying.
The central bank held rates steady, as most economists expected, and repeated its data-dependent guidance.
Northwind unveiled its next-generation AI accelerator chip, claiming twice the performance of rivals at similar power draw; preorders open next month.
Helios Renewables recalled 40,000 home batteries after reports of overheating, halting sales of its flagship product line.
Crestline Media will launch its ad-supported streaming tier in Europe in the second quarter.
Vertex Logistics will join the S&P 500 index next week, replacing a departing constituent.
Apex Retail's chief executive resigned abruptly after two years, the third senior departure this year; no successor was named.
BlueRiver Bancorp will move its headquarters to Charlotte next year; the bank said the relocation will not affect staffing levels.
Meridian Health beat quarterly estimates as sales of its new surgical robot doubled, and management raised its outlook.
Vertex Logistics missed earnings expectations and warned that freight demand remains weak as the industrial slowdown deepens.
Solaris Materials shares jumped after a rival submitted an unsolicited takeover bid at a 35% premium.
New tariffs on imported components will raise costs across the chip sector, Northwind warned, trimming its margin guidance.
Solaris Materials declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter.
Crestline Media acquired a small game studio to expand its interactive content lineup; terms were not disclosed.
